items.situation: asking a neighbor about noise or shared space
items.speaker: Didn't you hear that noise upstairs last night?

options.A: Was that keeping you up too?
options.B: The upstairs apartment has hardwood floors.
options.C: I'm painting my kitchen this weekend.
options.D: Yes, you're allowed to have guests over.

items.explanation: 正确答案 A 用一个反问推进对话，暗示自己也被吵到并确认对方的处境，是自然的口语回应。B 抓住 upstairs 这个词去描述楼上公寓的地板，答非所问（语义联想陷阱）。C 是一句完全无关、换个场景才成立的正常英语（跑题）。D 语法正确但回答的是'能不能带客人'这类许可问题，和是否听到噪音毫无关系（答错问题类型）。

items.situation: classmate asking to borrow lecture notes
items.speaker: What'd I miss during today's lecture?

options.A: The lecture hall was completely packed.
options.B: No worries, I've got you covered.
options.C: I'll meet you at the café later.

items.explanation: 正确答案 B 用习惯表达 'I've got you covered'（我罩着你/我帮你搞定）自然地表示愿意把笔记给对方，符合借笔记的语境。A 抓住 lecture 去描述教室有多挤，没有回应'错过了什么'（语义联想陷阱）。C 是换个场景才成立的无关句子（跑题）。D 回答的是上课时间（when），而对方问的是错过的内容（what），答错了问题类型。

items.speaker: How do I reschedule my appointment for Friday?

options.A: Your appointment reminder came by text yesterday.
options.B: My car's been making a strange sound.
options.C: Don't worry, I'll call and reschedule it.
options.D: The clinic's right next to the pharmacy.

items.explanation: 正确答案 C 不去解释'怎么改'，而是直接替对方解决底层需求——我来打电话改（语境转移）。A 抓住 appointment 去说提醒短信，没告诉对方怎么改（语义联想陷阱）。B 是完全无关、换个对话才成立的句子（跑题）。D 回答的是诊所在哪（where），而对方问的是怎么改约（how），答错了问题类型。

items.speaker: Where can I find your holiday hours listed?

options.A: Our busiest hours are in the morning.
options.B: I really enjoyed that sandwich yesterday.
options.C: Yes, we're hiring part-time staff now.
options.D: Actually, they're posted on our front door.

items.explanation: 正确答案 D 以话语标记 'Actually' 开头，间接地告诉对方去哪儿看假日营业时间——贴在前门上，切题且自然。A 抓住 hours 去说最忙的时段，没回答'在哪能看到'（语义联想陷阱）。B 是换个场景才成立的无关句子（跑题）。C 语法正确但回答的是'你们招不招人'这类问题，和查营业时间无关（答错问题类型）。

items.situation: student asking a professor about office hours or assignment
items.speaker: I'm not sure when your office hours are.

options.A: Well, I'm usually free after three.
options.B: My office is on the fourth floor.
options.C: The cafeteria changed its menu today.
options.D: Yes, the syllabus is posted online.

items.explanation: 正确答案 A 以话语标记 'Well' 开头，间接给出可以来找我的时间（三点后有空），正面回应了'不确定答疑时间'。B 抓住 office 去说办公室在几楼，回答的是地点而非时间（语义联想陷阱）。C 是换个场景才成立的无关句子（跑题）。D 语法正确但回答的是'教学大纲在不在网上'这类不同的是非问题，答错了问题类型。

items.situation: responding to an invitation or suggestion
items.speaker: We should all grab dinner after the show.

options.A: The restaurant added a new dessert menu.
options.C: My phone battery died this afternoon.
options.D: I already saw that show twice.

items.explanation: 正确答案 B 用习惯表达 'Count me in'（算我一个）热情地接受晚餐邀请，是自然的口语回应。A 抓住 dinner/restaurant 去描述餐厅新增甜点，没有回应邀请（语义联想陷阱）。C 是换个场景才成立的无关句子（跑题）。D 把 show 误当成'看过的一部剧'，其实这里指演出/活动，是词义误解陷阱，且没回应一起吃饭的提议。

items.situation: student asking about a reading list for a course
items.speaker: Why isn't the reading list posted online yet?

options.A: That reading was really long last week.
options.B: I left my laptop in the library.
options.C: Actually, the professor's still finalizing it.
options.D: It's on the second shelf over there.

items.explanation: 正确答案 C 以话语标记 'Actually' 开头，间接给出原因——教授还在定稿，正面回答了'为什么还没发'。A 抓住 reading 去评价上周阅读量大，答非所问（语义联想陷阱）。B 是换个场景才成立的无关句子（跑题）。D 回答的是书在哪个书架（where），而对方问的是为什么（why），答错了问题类型。

items.situation: student seeking help at the writing center
items.speaker: Can't someone help me revise my thesis?

options.A: The writing center has new computers.
options.B: I'm meeting my advisor at noon.
options.C: It's down the hall on your right.
options.D: Have you brought a draft with you?

items.explanation: 正确答案 D 用一个反问推进对话——你带草稿了吗，这是写作中心接待时自然的下一步。A 抓住 writing center 去说有新电脑，没有回应求助（语义联想陷阱）。B 是换个场景才成立的无关句子（跑题）。C 回答的是地点（往走廊右边走），而对方问的是能不能帮忙，答错了问题类型。
